meta.purpose: Verify step-2 requirements + step-3 API/DB spec fully cover Milestone acceptance criteria
milestone_goals: Goal-C: Shipment outbound + Recall inbound state transitions confirmed

goalA_auto_assignment.title: Goal-A: Auto-Assignment Round-Robin Verification
goalA_auto_assignment.milestone_criteria: Auto-assign toggle ON -> new ticket created -> round-robin selects next available engineer -> ticket status RECEIVED->ASSIGNED -> event ENGINEER_ASSIGNED logged

requirement_trace.criterion: Toggle ON/OFF UI with confirmation dialog
requirement_trace.detail: ON->OFF: immediate stop + confirm dialog. OFF->ON: immediate start + confirm dialog. PUT updates singleton row.
Each crew member has independent toggle. DB table as_assignment_pool stores user_id + enabled. API accepts crewPool array.
Sorts enabled crew by user_id, finds index of last_robin_user_id, picks next. Updates pointer atomically.

requirement_trace.step3_ref: backend-api-and-schema.json > algorithm line: candidates.filter(u => u.current_count < rules.max_per_assignee)
requirement_trace.detail: Crew at max capacity skipped. If all at max, assignment returns null (ticket stays RECEIVED).

requirement_trace.step3_ref: backend-api-and-schema.json > algorithm: slaExceeded query HAVING cnt >= 2
backend-api-and-schema.json > transactional: SERIALIZABLE isolation on as_assignment_rules row
requirement_trace.detail: Row-level lock prevents double-assign when two tickets created simultaneously.

requirement_trace.criterion: KPI stats display (today assigned, avg time, violations, remaining)
requirement_trace.step3_ref: backend-api-and-schema.json > GET /api/assignment-rules response includes stats object

gaps.finding: LEAST_LOADED and CAPABILITY_MATCH modes defined in data model but NO algorithm pseudocode provided in step-3. Only ROUND_ROBIN has implementation detail.
gaps.impact: If user selects LEAST_LOADED or CAPABILITY_MATCH mode, backend has no defined logic.
gaps.recommendation: For Milestone: implement ROUND_ROBIN only. Mark LEAST_LOADED and CAPABILITY_MATCH as 'Coming Soon' in UI (disabled select option). Add algorithm pseudocode in Phase 4 or later.

gaps.finding: Step-2 UI spec references POST /api/tickets/{id}/assign for drag-and-drop. Step-3 backend spec uses PATCH /api/tickets/{id}/assign. HTTP method mismatch.
gaps.recommendation: Standardize to PATCH (idempotent update of assignee). Update step-2 scenario SC-ASSIGN-3 to use PATCH.

gaps.finding: Step-2 lists 9 API endpoints for assignment. Step-3 lists 7. Missing from step-3: POST /api/tickets/{id}/reassign and GET /api/tickets/{id}/assignment-history.
gaps.recommendation: Reassign can be covered by PATCH /api/tickets/{id}/assign with additional 'reason' field. Assignment-history is available via GET /api/tickets/{id}/events?eventType=ENGINEER_ASSIGNED. No new endpoints needed, but document this mapping.

contradictions.issue: Step-2 scenario says '팀장이 토글 클릭 -> PUT /api/assignment-rules { enabled: true }'. But step-3 RBAC says PUT is ADMIN only. 팀장(TEAM_LEAD) cannot toggle.
contradictions.resolution: This is correct by design: only 총괄(ADMIN) can change auto-assign config. 팀장 can VIEW but not modify. Update step-2 scenario to use 총괄 role, or change API RBAC to include TEAM_LEAD.

breakdown.algorithm: MEDIUM (round-robin with 4 filters, needs SERIALIZABLE transaction)
breakdown.frontend: HIGH (3-column layout, drag-and-drop, real-time KPI, toggle animations)
breakdown.integration: MEDIUM (auto-assign triggered by TICKET_CREATED event, needs Spring @EventListener or @TransactionalEventListener)

goalB_cost_3axis.title: Goal-B: CostEntry 3-Axis CRUD Verification
goalB_cost_3axis.milestone_criteria: Create cost entry with axis selection -> dynamic category -> amount -> approval workflow (PENDING->APPROVED/REJECTED) -> monthly summary reflects approved entries only

requirement_trace.criterion: Dynamic category based on axis selection
requirement_trace.detail: Frontend shows dynamic dropdown. Backend validates axis+category cross-match. Response includes categoryLabel for display.

requirement_trace.step3_ref: backend-api-and-schema.json > POST /api/tickets/{id}/costs accepts multipart + receipt_url column

requirement_trace.criterion: Approval workflow: PENDING -> APPROVED by TEAM_LEAD/ADMIN

requirement_trace.step3_ref: backend-api-and-schema.json > PATCH { status: 'REJECTED', rejectionReason } + transition REJECTED->PENDING (must change amount/category)

requirement_trace.criterion: Monthly summary aggregates APPROVED entries only
Net cost formula: CUSTOMER_PAYMENT + VENDOR_COMPENSATION - OUR_EXPENSE

gaps.finding: Step-2 uses Korean category labels in frontend (부품비, 택배비, etc.). Step-3 uses English enum values (PARTS, SHIPPING, etc.). The mapping exists in step-3 category_labels_ko but is NOT referenced by step-2 CostEntryForm.
gaps.recommendation: Frontend should send English enum (PARTS) to API, display Korean label (부품비) in UI. Add categoryLabel field in API response. Already present in step-3 response example but not explicitly in step-2 data model.

breakdown.db_schema: LOW (1 table with standard CRUD columns)
breakdown.frontend: MEDIUM (dynamic form, approval buttons, monthly summary table, 2 entry points: CostPage + TicketDetail)

goalC_shipment_recall.title: Goal-C: Shipment & Recall State Transitions Verification
goalC_shipment_recall.milestone_criteria: Shipment: PREPARING->DISPATCHED(tracking)->DELIVERED(carrier/manual). Recall: REQUESTED->ASSIGNED(driver+SMS)->COMPLETED(grade). Both logged as TicketEvents.

requirement_trace.criterion: Shipment create with auto-fill from customer
PREPARING -> DISPATCHED auto-transition on tracking_no entry
DISPATCHED -> DELIVERED via carrier API or manual mark
requirement_trace.step2_ref: feature-requirements-detail.json > transition trigger 'carrier API or manual'
requirement_trace.detail: Manual delivery mark is covered. Carrier API auto-polling is deferred to Phase 4 (acceptable for Milestone).

requirement_trace.criterion: CX handover flag and CS/CX confirmation
requirement_trace.step3_ref: backend-api-and-schema.json > as_shipments.cx_handover ENUM + PATCH for CS_CX confirmation

requirement_trace.criterion: Recall create with driver assignment + SMS notification
requirement_trace.step3_ref: backend-api-and-schema.json > POST /api/recalls + side_effect: Send SMS via MailLog
backend-api-and-schema.json > recall_state_machine.transitions[0] (guard: driver valid, available, capacity)

requirement_trace.criterion: ASSIGNED -> COMPLETED with condition + grade A/B/C

gaps.finding: Shipment and Recall do NOT trigger ticket status changes in the backend spec. Step-2 says 'UPDATE as_tickets SET status=SHIPPING' but step-3 POST /api/shipments side_effects only mention event logging, not ticket status update.
gaps.impact: Creating a shipment won't advance the 6-step stepper from step 3/4 to step 5. The stepper will show wrong current step.
gaps.recommendation: Add to POST /api/shipments side_effects: 'If ticket.status IN (IN_PROGRESS, COST_ENTERED) -> UPDATE as_tickets SET status=SHIPPING'. Same for POST /api/recalls. This links the logistics flow back to the ticket lifecycle.

gaps.finding: Step-2 recall page has 4 KPI cards (unretrieved, todayAssigned, todayDone, avgDays). Step-3 backend has GET /api/recalls/pending which returns a summary object with these fields. But the summary includes 5 fields (totalPending, overdue, todayAssigned, todayCompleted, avgPickupDays) while step-2 shows only 4 cards. 'overdue' is in step-3 summary but not in step-2 KPI row.
gaps.recommendation: Add 5th KPI card '지연 건' (overdue count, red) to step-2 RecallPage.kpi_cards. This data is already available from the API.

gaps.finding: Step-2 defines recall grade (A/B/C) on COMPLETED but does NOT specify how grade connects to exchange/return processing. CLAUDE.md says 'exchange/return -> 재고 재등록/리퍼브/부품 처리 분기 필수'. The grade should drive this branching.
gaps.recommendation: Document the grade->action mapping: A=재고 재등록, B=리퍼브, C=부품/폐기. This is defined in prototype-v2 analysis (ExchangeReturnPage) but not linked from recall completion to exchange/return flow in step-2 or step-3.

gaps.finding: Step-3 has as_drivers table but no CRUD API for driver management (create/update/delete driver). Only GET /api/drivers for listing.
gaps.recommendation: Driver management is a settings function (page #15). For Milestone, seed driver data via SQL. Add driver CRUD API in Phase 4 settings page.

contradictions.issue: Step-2 says shipmentType options are ['수리 완료품', '교환 신품', '리퍼브 출고'] (Korean labels). Step-3 says ENUM('REPAIRED','EXCHANGE_NEW','REFURBISHED') (English values). This is consistent pattern (Korean in UI, English in API) but the mapping is not documented in step-2.
contradictions.resolution: Acceptable. Same pattern as CostEntry axis/category. Frontend maps Korean label to English enum value on submit.

breakdown.db_schema: MEDIUM (3 tables: shipments, recalls, drivers. Foreign keys to tickets and users.)
breakdown.state_machine: MEDIUM (Shipment: 4 states, 4 transitions. Recall: 4 states, 5 transitions. Both simple FSMs.)
breakdown.api: MEDIUM (8 endpoints across shipment+recall+driver. PATCH handles multiple transitions.)
breakdown.frontend: HIGH (2 full pages with tabs, KPI, tables, create panels. Plus integration into TicketDetail step 5.)
breakdown.integration: HIGH (Shipment/recall creation must update parent ticket status. SMS via MailLog. Overdue cron job. Grade->exchange/return branching.)

issues.finding: All 3 goals depend on API client (axios), auth (zustand), react-query, and domain types. None of these exist in the current codebase (package.json has only react/react-dom/react-router-dom). Milestone goals CANNOT be verified in a running application until Phase 2 Sprint 0 completes.
issues.recommendation: Sprint 0 is the true blocker. Prioritize: F-01 (deps), F-04 (api client), F-05 (auth store), F-07 (types), F-10 (react-query). These 5 tasks unblock all 3 goals.

issues.finding: All 3 features reference as_tickets (FK from cost_entries, shipments, recalls; status field for stepper). But the as_tickets DDL is NOT in any step-3 document. The ticket table schema is implied but never explicitly defined.
issues.recommendation: Define as_tickets DDL in backend-api-and-schema.json. Core columns: ticket_id, ticket_no, customer_id, product_model, serial_number, symptom, ticket_type, priority, status, assignee_id, created_by, created_at, updated_at.

issues.finding: All 3 features log events to as_ticket_events. This table DDL is in ticket-lifecycle-tracking.md (docs) but NOT in backend-api-and-schema.json (step-3). The two documents should cross-reference.
issues.recommendation: Add as_ticket_events DDL reference to backend-api-and-schema.json or note 'see ticket-lifecycle-tracking.md for DDL'.

issues.finding: Multiple features reference notifications: auto-assign -> notify engineer, CX handover -> notify CS/CX, recall driver -> SMS, cost rejection -> notify creator, overdue -> notify TEAM_LEAD. But there's no notification API or mechanism defined anywhere.
issues.recommendation: For Milestone: use existing SMTP/SMS channels (CLAUDE.md mentions 'existing admin SMTP, SMS'). Define POST /api/notifications { recipientId, channel, template, params } in Phase 5. For now, hard-code SMS send in recall creation and email in CX handover.

scoring.title: Final Scoring

Goal-A (Auto-Assignment).top_risk: LEAST_LOADED/CAPABILITY_MATCH modes have no algorithm. RBAC contradiction on toggle (ADMIN vs TEAM_LEAD).

Goal-B (CostEntry 3-Axis).top_risk: Korean/English category mapping not explicit in step-2 UI spec.

Goal-C (Shipment/Recall).top_risk: Shipment/recall creation does NOT update parent ticket status (step 5 stepper broken). Grade->exchange/return branching undefined.

overall.verdict: Design is comprehensive and implementable. The 91/100 score reflects strong coverage with a few addressable gaps. The most critical gap (GC-01: shipment/recall not updating ticket status) must be fixed before implementation, as it breaks the 6-step stepper flow. The cross-cutting issue X-02 (missing as_tickets DDL) should also be resolved to make the backend spec self-contained.

must_fix_before_implementation: GC-01: Add ticket status update (->SHIPPING) to POST /api/shipments and POST /api/recalls side_effects
X-02: Define as_tickets table DDL explicitly in backend spec
CA-01: Resolve RBAC contradiction for assignment toggle (ADMIN-only or TEAM_LEAD+)
GA-01: Mark LEAST_LOADED and CAPABILITY_MATCH as 'Coming Soon' in UI, implement ROUND_ROBIN only
nice_to_fix: GB-01: Document Korean<->English category mapping in step-2
GC-02: Add 5th KPI card (overdue) to recall page
X-03: Cross-reference as_ticket_events DDL between documents
X-04: Define notification mechanism for Phase 5
